chapter.title: Character Classes
chapter.description: Characters' capabilities are largely determined by class. This chapter offers twelve classes, along with forty-eight subclasses. If you're a player, consult with your DM before using a class's sidebar, which might or might not be suitable for your campaign.

classes.name: Barbarian
classes.tagline: A Fierce Warrior of Primal Rage
classes.flavor_text:
Barbarians are mighty warriors who are powered by primal forces of the multiverse that manifest as a Rage. More than a mere emotion — and not limited to anger — this Rage is an incarnation of a predator's ferocity, a storm's fury, and a sea's turmoil.

Some Barbarians personify their Rage as a fierce spirit or revered forebear. Others see it as a connection to the pain and anguish of the world, as an impersonal tangle of wild magic, or as an expression of their own deepest self. For every Barbarian, their Rage is a power that fuels not just battle prowess, but also uncanny reflexes and heightened senses.

Barbarians often serve as protectors and leaders in their communities. They charge headlong into danger so those under their protection don't have to. Their courage in the face of danger makes Barbarians perfectly suited for adventure.

instructions: Gain all the traits in the Core Barbarian Traits table.
Gain the Barbarian's level 1 features, which are listed in the Barbarian Features table.
Gain the following traits from the Core Barbarian Traits table: Hit Point Die, proficiency with Martial weapons, and training with Shields.
Gain the Barbarian's level 1 features, which are listed in the Barbarian Features table.

class_features.name: Rage
class_features.description: You can imbue yourself with a primal power called Rage, a force that grants you extraordinary might and resilience. You can enter it as a Bonus Action if you aren't wearing Heavy armor.

resource.uses_per_level: See Rages column in Barbarian Features table

damage_resistance.description: You have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

rage_damage.description: When you make an attack using Strength — with either a weapon or an Unarmed Strike — and deal damage to the target, you gain a bonus to the damage that increases as you gain levels as a Barbarian, as shown in the Rage Damage column of the Barbarian Features table.
rage_damage.trigger: Attack using Strength with weapon or Unarmed Strike that deals damage

strength_advantage.description: You have Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.

no_concentration_or_spells.description: You can't maintain Concentration, and you can't cast spells.

duration.base: Until the end of your next turn

extension.description: If your Rage is still active on your next turn, you can extend the Rage for another round by doing one of the following:
options: Make an attack roll against an enemy.
Force an enemy to make a saving throw.
Take a Bonus Action to extend your Rage.
extension.extended_duration: Until the end of your next turn

class_features.name: Unarmored Defense
class_features.description: While you aren't wearing any armor, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Constitution modifiers. You can use a Shield and still gain this benefit.

class_features.name: Weapon Mastery
class_features.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice, such as Greataxes and Handaxes. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.
class_features.scaling: See Weapon Mastery column of Barbarian Features table

class_features.name: Danger Sense
class_features.description: You gain an uncanny sense of when things aren't as they should be, giving you an edge when you dodge perils. You have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws unless you have the Incapacitated condition.
class_features.exception: Unless you have the Incapacitated condition

class_features.name: Reckless Attack
class_features.description: You can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with increased ferocity. When you make your first attack roll on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you Advantage on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn, but attack rolls against you have Advantage during that time.
class_features.trigger: When you make your first attack roll on your turn
class_features.benefit: Advantage on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn
class_features.drawback: Attack rolls against you have Advantage during that time

class_features.name: Barbarian Subclass
class_features.description: You gain a Barbarian subclass of your choice. The Path of the Berserker, Path of the Wild Heart, Path of the World Tree, and Path of the Zealot subclasses are detailed after this class's description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Barbarian levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Barbarian level or lower.

class_features.name: Primal Knowledge
class_features.description: You gain proficiency in another skill of your choice from the skill list available to Barbarians at level 1.

rage_channel.description: While your Rage is active, you can channel primal power when you attempt certain tasks; whenever you make an ability check using one of the following skills, you can make it as a Strength check even if it normally uses a different ability: Acrobatics, Intimidation, Perception, Stealth, or Survival.
rage_channel.flavor: When you use this ability, your Strength represents primal power coursing through you, honing your agility, bearing, and senses.

class_features.name: Ability Score Improvement
class_features.description: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat (see chapter 5) or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Barbarian levels 8, 12, and 16.

class_features.name: Extra Attack
class_features.description: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

class_features.name: Fast Movement
class_features.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing Heavy armor.

class_features.name: Feral Instinct
class_features.description: Your instincts are so honed that you have Advantage on Initiative rolls.

class_features.name: Instinctive Pounce
class_features.description: As part of the Bonus Action you take to enter your Rage, you can move up to half your Speed.
class_features.trigger: When entering Rage as Bonus Action
class_features.benefit: Move up to half your Speed

class_features.name: Brutal Strike
class_features.description: If you use Reckless Attack, you can forgo any Advantage on one Strength-based attack roll of your choice on your turn. The chosen attack roll mustn't have Disadvantage. If the chosen attack roll hits, the target takes an extra 1d10 damage of the same type dealt by the weapon or Unarmed Strike, and you can cause one Brutal Strike effect of your choice.
class_features.cost: Forgo Advantage on one Strength-based attack roll
class_features.restriction: Attack roll must not have Disadvantage
class_features.extra_damage: 1d10 (same type as weapon or Unarmed Strike)

effect_options.name: Forceful Blow
effect_options.description: The target is pushed 15 feet straight away from you. You can then move up to half your Speed straight toward the target without provoking Opportunity Attacks.
effect_options.self_movement: Up to half Speed straight toward target

effect_options.name: Hamstring Blow
effect_options.description: The target's Speed is reduced by 15 feet until the start of your next turn. A target can be affected by only one Hamstring Blow at a time — the most recent one.
effect_options.duration: Until start of your next turn

class_features.name: Relentless Rage
class_features.description: Your Rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 Hit Points while your Rage is active and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, your Hit Points instead change to a number equal to twice your Barbarian level.
class_features.trigger: Drop to 0 HP while Rage is active and don't die outright
class_features.success_effect: Hit Points change to twice your Barbarian level

class_features.name: Improved Brutal Strike (Level 13)
class_features.description: You have honed new ways to attack furiously. The following effects are now among your Brutal Strike options.

new_effect_options.name: Staggering Blow
new_effect_options.description: The target has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes, and it can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of your next turn.

new_effect_options.name: Sundering Blow
new_effect_options.description: Before the start of your next turn, the next attack roll made by another creature against the target gains a +5 bonus to the roll. An attack roll can gain only one Sundering Blow bonus.

class_features.name: Persistent Rage
class_features.description_part1: When you roll Initiative, you can regain all expended uses of Rage. After you regain uses of Rage in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
class_features.description_part2: In addition, your Rage is so fierce that it now lasts for 10 minutes without you needing to do anything to extend it from round to round. Your Rage ends early if you have the Unconscious condition (not just the Incapacitated condition) or don Heavy armor.

initiative_rage_recovery.effect: Regain all expended uses of Rage

class_features.name: Improved Brutal Strike (Level 17)
class_features.description: The extra damage of your Brutal Strike increases to 2d10. In addition, you can use two different Brutal Strike effects whenever you use your Brutal Strike feature.

class_features.name: Indomitable Might
class_features.description: If your total for a Strength check or Strength saving throw is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

class_features.name: Epic Boon
class_features.description: You gain an Epic Boon feat (see chapter 5) or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. Boon of Irresistible Offense is recommended.

class_features.name: Primal Champion
class_features.description: You embody primal power. Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

subclasses.name: Path of the Berserker
subclasses.tagline: Channel Rage into Violent Fury
subclasses.description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Berserker direct their Rage primarily toward violence. Their path is one of untrammeled fury, and they thrill in the chaos of battle as they allow their Rage to seize and empower them.

features.name: Frenzy
features.description: If you use Reckless Attack while your Rage is active, you deal extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack. To determine the extra damage, roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. The damage has the same type as the weapon or Unarmed Strike used for the attack.
features.prerequisite: Reckless Attack while Rage is active
features.damage_type: Same as weapon or Unarmed Strike

features.name: Mindless Rage
features.description: You have Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions while your Rage is active. If you're Charmed or Frightened when you enter your Rage, the condition ends on you.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike.
features.trigger: Take damage from a creature within 5 feet
features.attack: One melee attack (weapon or Unarmed Strike)

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can strike terror into others with your menacing presence and primal power. When you do so, each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, a creature has the Frightened condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of the Frightened creature's turns, the creature repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.
features.repeat_save: End of each of Frightened creature's turns
features.recharge: Long Rest or expend a use of Rage (no action required)

subclasses.name: Path of the Wild Heart
subclasses.tagline: Walk in Community with the Animal World
subclasses.description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the Wild Heart view themselves as kin to animals. These Barbarians learn magical means to communicate with animals, and their Rage heightens their connection to animals as it fills them with supernatural might.

features.name: Animal Speaker
features.description: You can cast the Beast Sense and Speak with Animals spells but only as Rituals. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them.

features.name: Rage of the Wilds
features.description: Your Rage taps into the primal power of animals. Whenever you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice.
features.trigger: On Rage activation

options.name: Bear
options.description: While your Rage is active, you have Resistance to every damage type except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant.
options.resistances: All except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant

options.name: Eagle
options.description: When you activate your Rage, you can take the Disengage and Dash actions as part of that Bonus Action. While your Rage is active, you can take a Bonus Action to take both of those actions.
options.on_activation: Disengage and Dash as part of Rage Bonus Action
options.while_active: Bonus Action for Disengage and Dash

options.name: Wolf
options.description: While your Rage is active, your allies have Advantage on attack rolls against any enemy of yours within 5 feet of you.
options.benefit: Allies have Advantage on attacks vs enemies within 5 feet of you

features.name: Aspect of the Wilds
features.description: You gain one of the following options of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your choice.

options.name: Owl
options.description: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet.
options.stacks: If already have Darkvision, range increases by 60 feet

options.name: Panther
options.description: You have a Climb Speed equal to your Speed.

options.name: Salmon
options.description: You have a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

features.name: Nature Speaker
features.description: You can cast the Commune with Nature spell but only as a Ritual. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for it.

features.name: Power of the Wilds
features.description: Whenever you activate your Rage, you gain one of the following options of your choice.
features.trigger: On Rage activation

options.name: Falcon
options.description: While your Rage is active, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed if you aren't wearing any armor.

options.name: Lion
options.description: While your Rage is active, any of your enemies within 5 feet of you have Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you or another Barbarian who has this option active.

options.name: Ram
options.description: While your Rage is active, you can cause a Large or smaller creature to have the Prone condition when you hit it with a melee attack.

subclasses.name: Path of the World Tree
subclasses.tagline: Trace the Roots and Branches of the Multiverse
subclasses.description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the World Tree connect with the cosmic tree Yggdrasil through their Rage. This tree grows among the Outer Planes, connecting them to each other and the Material Plane. These Barbarians draw on the tree's magic for vitality and as a means of dimensional travel.

features.name: Vitality of the Tree
features.description: Your Rage taps into the life force of the World Tree. You gain the following benefits.

benefits.name: Vitality Surge
benefits.description: When you activate your Rage, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Barbarian level.

benefits.name: Life-Giving Force
benefits.description: At the start of each of your turns while your Rage is active, you can choose another creature within 10 feet of yourself to gain Temporary Hit Points. To determine the number of Temporary Hit Points, roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. If any of these Temporary Hit Points remain when your Rage ends, they vanish.

features.name: Branches of the Tree
features.description: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a Reaction to summon spectral branches of the World Tree around it. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or be teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of yourself or in the nearest unoccupied space you can see. After the target teleports, you can reduce its Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn.
features.trigger: Creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet while Rage active
features.failed_save_effect: Teleported to space within 5 feet of you
features.additional_effect: Can reduce target Speed to 0 until end of current turn

features.name: Battering Roots
features.description: During your turn, your reach is 10 feet greater with any Melee weapon that has the Heavy or Versatile property, as tendrils of the World Tree extend from you. When you hit with such a weapon on your turn, you can activate the Push or Topple mastery property in addition to a different mastery property you're using with that weapon.

features.name: Travel along the Tree
features.description: When you activate your Rage and as a Bonus Action while your Rage is active, you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

bring_allies.destination: Unoccupied space within 10 feet of your destination

subclasses.name: Path of the Zealot
subclasses.tagline: Rage in Ecstatic Union with a God
subclasses.description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Zealot receive boons from a god or pantheon. These Barbarians experience their Rage as an ecstatic episode of divine union that infuses them with power. They are often allies to the priests and other followers of their god or pantheon.

features.name: Divine Fury
features.description: You can channel divine power into your strikes. On each of your turns while your Rage is active, the first creature you hit with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike takes extra damage equal to 1d6 plus half your Barbarian level (round down). The extra damage is Necrotic or Radiant; you choose the type each time you deal the damage.
features.frequency: First creature hit each turn while Rage active

features.name: Warrior of the Gods
features.description: A divine entity helps ensure you can continue the fight. You have a pool of four d12s that you can spend to heal yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can expend dice from the pool, roll them, and regain a number of Hit Points equal to the roll's total.

features.name: Fanatical Focus
features.description: Once per active Rage, if you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a bonus equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and you must use the new roll.

features.name: Zealous Presence
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you unleash a battle cry infused with divine energy. Up to ten other creatures of your choice within 60 feet of you gain Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn.
features.targets: Up to 10 other creatures of your choice within 60 feet
features.benefit: Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws until start of your next turn
features.recharge: Long Rest or expend a use of Rage (no action required)

features.name: Rage of the Gods
features.description: When you activate your Rage, you can assume the form of a divine warrior. This form lasts for 1 minute or until you drop to 0 Hit Points. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
features.duration: 1 minute or until drop to 0 HP

benefits.name: Flight
benefits.description: You have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed and can hover.

benefits.name: Resistance
benefits.description: You have Resistance to Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant damage.

benefits.name: Revivification
benefits.description: When a creature within 30 feet of you would drop to 0 Hit Points, you can take a Reaction to expend a use of your Rage to instead change the target's Hit Points to a number equal to your Barbarian level.

classes.name: Bard
classes.tagline: An Inspiring Performer of Music, Dance, and Magic
classes.flavor_text:
Invoking magic through music, dance, and verse, Bards are expert at inspiring others, soothing hurts, disheartening foes, and creating illusions. Bards believe the multiverse was spoken into existence and that remnants of its Words of Creation still resound and glimmer on every plane of existence. Bardic magic attempts to harness those words, which transcend any language.

Anything can inspire a new song or tale, so Bards are fascinated by almost everything. They become masters of many things, including performing music, working magic, and making jests.

A Bard's life is spent traveling, gathering lore, telling stories, and living on the gratitude of audiences, much like any other entertainer. But Bards' depth of knowledge and mastery of magic sets them apart.

instructions: Gain all the traits in the Core Bard Traits table.
Gain the Bard's level 1 features, which are listed in the Bard Features table.

Gain the following traits from the Core Bard Traits table: Hit Point Die, proficiency in one skill of your choice, proficiency with one Musical Instrument of your choice, and training with Light armor.
Gain the Bard's level 1 features, which are listed in the Bard Features table. See the multiclassing rules in chapter 2 to determine your available spell slots.

class_features.name: Bardic Inspiration
class_features.description: You can supernaturally inspire others through words, music, or dance. This inspiration is represented by your Bardic Inspiration die, which is a d6.

usage.target: Another creature within 60 feet who can see or hear you
usage.effect: Target gains one Bardic Inspiration die. Once within the next hour when the creature fails a D20 Test, it can roll the die and add it to the d20, potentially turning failure into success.
usage.limit_per_creature: One Bardic Inspiration die at a time

class_features.name: Spellcasting
class_features.description: You have learned to cast spells through your bardic arts.

cantrips.replacement: Whenever you gain a Bard level, replace one cantrip with another from Bard list

prepared_spells.changing: Whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Bard spell for which you have spell slots.

class_features.name: Expertise
class_features.description: You gain Expertise in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. Performance and Persuasion are recommended if you have proficiency in them.

class_features.name: Jack of All Trades
class_features.description: You can add half your Proficiency Bonus (round down) to any ability check you make that uses a skill proficiency you lack and that doesn't otherwise use your Proficiency Bonus.
class_features.applies_to: Ability checks using skills you lack proficiency in that don't otherwise use Proficiency Bonus

class_features.name: Font of Inspiration
class_features.description:
You now regain all your expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

In addition, you can expend a spell slot (no action required) to regain one expended use of Bardic Inspiration.
class_features.spell_slot_conversion: Expend a spell slot to regain one use of Bardic Inspiration (no action required)

class_features.name: Countercharm
class_features.description: You can use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects. If you or a creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw against an effect that applies the Charmed or Frightened condition, you can take a Reaction to cause the save to be rerolled, and the new roll has Advantage.
class_features.trigger: You or creature within 30 feet fails save against Charmed or Frightened effect

class_features.name: Magical Secrets
class_features.description: You've learned secrets from various magical traditions. Whenever you reach a Bard level (including this level) and the Prepared Spells number in the Bard Features table increases, you can choose any of your new prepared spells from the Bard, Cleric, Druid, and Wizard spell lists, and the chosen spells count as Bard spells for you. In addition, whenever you replace a spell prepared for this class, you can replace it with a spell from those lists.

class_features.name: Superior Inspiration
class_features.description: When you roll Initiative, you regain expended uses of Bardic Inspiration until you have two if you have fewer than that.

class_features.name: Words of Creation
class_features.description: You have mastered two of the Words of Creation: the words of life and death. You therefore always have the Power Word Heal and Power Word Kill spells prepared. When you cast either spell, you can target a second creature with it if that creature is within 10 feet of the first target.

sidebar.title: A Bard's Repertoire
sidebar.content:
Does your Bard beat a drum while chanting the deeds of ancient heroes? Strum a lute while crooning romantic tunes? Perform arias of stirring power? Recite dramatic monologues from classic tragedies? Use the rhythm of a folk dance to coordinate the movement of allies in battle? Compose naughty limericks?

When you play a Bard, consider the style of artistic performance you favor, the moods you might invoke, and the themes that inspire your own creations. Are your poems inspired by moments of natural beauty, or are they brooding reflections on loss? Do you prefer lofty hymns or rowdy tavern songs? Are you drawn to laments for the fallen or celebrations of joy? Do you dance merry jigs or perform elaborate interpretive choreography? Do you focus on one style of performance or strive to master them all?

subclasses.name: College of Dance
subclasses.tagline: Move in Harmony with the Cosmos
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Dance know that the Words of Creation can't be contained within speech or song; the words are uttered by the movements of celestial bodies and flow through the motions of the smallest creatures. These Bards practice a way of being in harmony with the whirling cosmos that emphasizes agility, speed, and grace.

features.name: Dazzling Footwork
features.restriction: While not wearing armor or wielding a Shield

benefits.name: Dance Virtuoso
benefits.description: You have Advantage on any Charisma (Performance) check you make that involves you dancing.

benefits.name: Agile Strikes
benefits.description: When you expend a use of your Bardic Inspiration as part of an action, a Bonus Action, or a Reaction, you can make one Unarmed Strike as part of that action, Bonus Action, or Reaction.

benefits.name: Bardic Damage
benefits.description: You can use Dexterity instead of Strength for the attack rolls of your Unarmed Strikes. When you deal damage with an Unarmed Strike, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die plus your Dexterity modifier, instead of the strike's normal damage. This roll doesn't expend the die.

features.name: Inspiring Movement
features.trigger: When an enemy you can see ends its turn within 5 feet of you
features.effect: Move up to half your Speed. Then one ally within 30 feet can also move up to half their Speed using their Reaction.

features.name: Tandem Footwork
features.trigger: When you roll Initiative
features.effect: Roll Bardic Inspiration die; you and each ally within 30 feet who can see or hear you gains bonus to Initiative equal to the number rolled.

features.name: Leading Evasion
features.description: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and only half damage if you fail. If any creatures within 5 feet of you are making the same Dexterity saving throw, you can share this benefit with them for that save.

subclasses.name: College of Glamour
subclasses.tagline: Weave Beguiling Fey Magic
subclasses.description: The College of Glamour traces its origins to the beguiling magic of the Feywild. Bards who study this magic weave threads of beauty and terror into their songs and stories, and the mightiest among them can cloak themselves in otherworldly majesty. Their performances stir wistful longing for forgotten innocence, evoke unconscious memories of long-held fears, and tug at the emotions of even the most hard-hearted listeners.

additional_effect.trigger: Immediately after you cast an Enchantment or Illusion spell using a spell slot
additional_effect.target: A creature you can see within 60 feet
additional_effect.failed_save: Charmed or Frightened (your choice) for 1 minute
additional_effect.repeat_save: End of each of its turns
additional_effect.recharge: Long Rest or expend one use of Bardic Inspiration (no action required)

features.name: Mantle of Inspiration
features.targets: Up to Charisma modifier creatures within 60 feet (minimum one)
features.effect: Each gains Temporary Hit Points equal to two times the Bardic Inspiration die roll, then each can use Reaction to move up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

features.name: Mantle of Majesty
features.effect: Cast Command without expending a spell slot, take on unearthly appearance for 1 minute or until Concentration ends. During this time, can cast Command as a Bonus Action without spell slot.
features.charmed_auto_fail: Any creature Charmed by you automatically fails its save against Command cast with this feature.
features.recharge: Long Rest or expend a level 3+ spell slot (no action required)

features.name: Unbreakable Majesty
features.effect: Whenever any creature hits you with an attack roll for the first time on a turn, the attacker must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC, or the attack misses instead.

subclasses.name: College of Lore
subclasses.tagline: Plumb the Depths of Magical Knowledge
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Lore collect spells and secrets from diverse sources, such as scholarly tomes, mystical rites, and peasant tales. The college's members gather in libraries and universities to share their lore with one another. They also meet at festivals or affairs of state, where they can expose corruption, unravel lies, and poke fun at self-important figures of authority.

features.name: Cutting Words
features.trigger: When a creature you can see within 60 feet makes a damage roll or succeeds on an ability check or attack roll
features.effect: Roll Bardic Inspiration die, subtract from creature's roll, reducing damage or potentially turning success into failure.

features.name: Magical Discoveries
features.spell_restriction: Cantrip or spell for which you have spell slots
features.replacement: Whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one spell with another meeting requirements.

features.name: Peerless Skill
features.trigger: When you make an ability check or attack roll and fail
features.effect: Roll Bardic Inspiration die, add to d20, potentially turning failure into success.

subclasses.name: College of Valor
subclasses.tagline: Sing the Deeds of Ancient Heroes
subclasses.description: Bards of the College of Valor are daring storytellers whose tales preserve the memory of the great heroes of the past. These Bards sing the deeds of the mighty in vaulted halls or to crowds gathered around great bonfires. They travel to witness great events firsthand and to ensure that the memory of these events doesn't pass away. With their songs, they inspire new generations to reach the same heights of accomplishment as the heroes of old.

features.name: Combat Inspiration
features.description: A creature that has a Bardic Inspiration die from you can use it for one of the following effects.

options.name: Defense
options.effect: Roll Bardic Inspiration die and add to AC against that attack, potentially causing miss.

options.name: Offense
options.trigger: Immediately after hitting a target with an attack roll
options.effect: Roll Bardic Inspiration die and add to attack's damage.

features.name: Martial Training
features.spellcasting_focus: Simple or Martial weapon can be used as Spellcasting Focus for Bard spells

features.name: Extra Attack
features.cantrip_replacement: Can cast one cantrip with casting time of an action in place of one attack

features.name: Battle Magic

classes.name: Cleric
classes.tagline: A Miraculous Priest of Divine Power
classes.flavor_text:
Clerics draw power from the realms of the gods and harness it to work miracles. Blessed by a deity, a pantheon, or another immortal entity, a Cleric can reach out to the divine magic of the Outer Planes — where gods dwell — and channel it to bolster people and battle foes.

Because their power is a divine gift, Clerics typically associate themselves with temples dedicated to the deity or other immortal force that unlocked their magic. Harnessing divine magic doesn't rely on specific training, yet Clerics might learn prayers and rites that help them draw on power from the Outer Planes.

Not every member of a temple or shrine is a Cleric. Some priests are called to a simple life of temple service, carrying out their devotion through prayer and rituals, not through magic. Many mortals claim to speak for the gods, but few can marshal the power of those gods the way a Cleric can.

instructions: Gain the following traits from the Core Cleric Traits table: Hit Point Die and training with Light and Medium armor and Shields.
Gain the Cleric's level 1 features. See the multiclassing rules in chapter 2 to determine your available spell slots.

sidebar.title: Breaking Your Oath
sidebar.content:
A Paladin tries to hold to the highest standards of conduct, but even the most dedicated are fallible. Sometimes a Paladin transgresses their oath.

A Paladin who has broken a vow typically seeks absolution, spending an all-night vigil as a sign of penitence or undertaking a fast. After a rite of forgiveness, the Paladin starts fresh.

If your Paladin unrepentantly violates their oath, talk to your DM. Your Paladin should probably take a more appropriate subclass or even abandon the class and adopt another one.
